Entity_Description: Strawberry Hill is a neighbourhood in Surrey, British Columbia, within Metro Vancouver, centred along the Scott Road (120 Street) corridor that divides Surrey from Delta. Home to roughly 41,000 residents, the area takes its name from the strawberry farms that covered these slopes in the early twentieth century. Today it is a busy commercial and residential district anchored by the Strawberry Hill Shopping Centre, with a housing mix of established single-family homes, basement-entry houses and newer townhome infill. It sits about 24 kilometres southeast of downtown Vancouver, above the low-lying Fraser River delta.

Short_Description: A century ago, Strawberry Hill really was a hill of strawberries — farm fields sloping toward the Fraser River delta gave this Surrey neighbourhood its name. Those berry farms are long gone, replaced by one of Metro Vancouver's busiest crossroads: the Scott Road corridor, the Strawberry Hill Shopping Centre, and street after street of established family homes now joined by townhome infill. Around 41,000 people call this area home, and its aging housing stock works hard through wet Lower Mainland winters. Long_Description:
Strawberry Hill wears its history in its name. In the early 1900s these slopes above the Fraser River delta were covered in strawberry fields, worked by some of Surrey's earliest farming families. The berries are gone, but the hill remains one of the most recognizable corners of Surrey — the high ground along Scott Road (120 Street), where Surrey meets Delta and where roughly 41,000 people now live, shop and commute.

That geography matters for {SERVICE}. Properties here sit on the rise between the low-lying delta soils to the west and Surrey's newer master-planned neighbourhoods to the east, and the housing stock tells the story of decades of growth: basement-entry homes from the 1970s and 80s, many with secondary suites, alongside newer townhome complexes and strata developments that reflect Metro Vancouver's shift to multi-family living. Each era of building brings its own quirks, and {COMPANY_NAME} has seen most of them.

Then there is the weather. Strawberry Hill shares Metro Vancouver's mild oceanic climate — warm, dry summers followed by a November-to-March stretch when rain arrives in volume. That sustained winter moisture drives the drainage, moss and algae issues that show up on roofs, siding and hardscaping across this neighbourhood's older homes. A {BUSINESS_TYPE} working here needs to plan around the wet season, not be surprised by it.

The commercial side of Strawberry Hill is just as demanding. The Strawberry Hill Shopping Centre and the Scott Road corridor form one of Surrey's busiest retail strips, and the surrounding blocks mix storefronts, services and high-traffic parking areas with residential streets. {COMPANY_NAME} serves both sides of that line — {PROPERTY_TYPE} properties on the quiet streets and the businesses that keep 120 Street moving.

Coverage is straightforward from here. Central Surrey is about 7 kilometres away, New Westminster and Bridgeview roughly 8, and Fraser Heights about 10, so {RESPONSE_TIME} response is realistic across the whole area, with downtown Vancouver only 24 kilometres out. {SERVICE_DESCRIPTION}

What sets {COMPANY_NAME} apart in Strawberry Hill is simple: {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP}. Backed by {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of experience and a {RATING} rating, we treat this neighbourhood's older homes and busy commercial frontage with the same care.
